core_mechanism: topological (homotopy-class) classification of line singularities in a continuously-ordered field, generated by a cut-and-relative-displacement (Volterra) construction and extended to fold in the field's relaxation/reorganization degrees of freedom rather than only relaxation-stable invariants
brief_reason: The abstract characterizes line defects via an 'extended Volterra process' that accounts for relaxation of a medium with continuous symmetries and contrasts it with the 'topological theory of line defects' (stable-against-relaxation invariants), presenting the latter as a simplified special case of the cut-and-displacement construction.
